label: Coverage Canvas
prompt: Build a dark-theme canvas dashboard showing the current backend unit test coverage status for shogo-labs/shogo-ai on branch fix(coverage)/backend-unitTests. Clone the repo, read README.md (coverage badges), .shogo/plans/backend-100-coverage-finale_v2.plan.md, and coverage/ (thresholds.json, coverage-tasks.json, baselines/*.gaps.json), then build src/components/CoverageDashboard.tsx with: overall backend + frontend % KPIs, per-package cards for ALL 11 backend packages in the v2 expanded aggregate (apps/api, packages/agent-runtime, packages/sdk, packages/shared-runtime, packages/model-catalog, packages/voice, packages/email, packages/agent, packages/core, packages/cli, packages/shogo-worker) with line% + function% bars vs thresholds, wave breakdown (Wave A/B/C/D/E task counts and target %), aggregate-trend sparkline from sprintCommits[], and a 'Top Gaps' list of the largest remaining uncovered files. Use recharts + shadcn cards + zinc-950 dark theme. Mount it from src/App.tsx.

label: Execute Plan
prompt: Aggressively execute the next pending wave-task from .shogo/plans/backend-100-coverage-finale_v2.plan.md against the shogo-labs/shogo-ai repo on branch fix(coverage)/backend-unitTests ONLY (never main, never any other branch). Steps: (1) cd /tmp/shogo-ai && git checkout 'fix(coverage)/backend-unitTests' && git pull --rebase. (2) Read the v2 plan. If Wave A Day 1 (stabilize broken tests) is NOT yet complete, do that first — fix shared-runtime env leaks, sdk Playwright stray files, agent-runtime hangs, apps/api core dumps — measure with a clean bun test run that has zero failures. Otherwise pick the next pending Wave/Day target. (3) Implement it end-to-end: write the unit tests, mock dependencies (memfs / mock.module / child_process / in-memory LSP / sqlite test db / @aws-sdk / playwright-core / @anthropic-ai/sdk / @opentelemetry/api / @elevenlabs as appropriate), run `bun test --coverage` for the affected package, verify the target file(s) hit ≥95% lines and ≥99% functions. (4) BATCH AGGRESSIVELY: if the wave-task is a sweep across multiple small files, complete 2-4 files in one session and one commit — don't fragment. (5) If anything is unreachable dead code (defensive guards on provably-never-falsy values, redundant null checks, etc.), DELETE it instead of testing it and note it in the commit message. (6) git add -A && git commit -m 'test(coverage): <file(s)> — <before>% → <after>% lines' && git push origin 'fix(coverage)/backend-unitTests'. (7) Update sprintCommits[] in coverage/baselines/_aggregate.gaps.json with the new commit + per-file delta. (8) Report new aggregate coverage % (in the v2 expanded aggregate that includes all 11 backend packages), the wave checkpoint progress, and what the next task is. Do NOT push to any other branch. Do NOT open PRs. Do NOT touch main. NEVER mark a task done if it introduced new test failures.

label: Sync Readme
prompt: Recompute backend coverage % from actual coverage data on branch fix(coverage)/backend-unitTests using the v2 EXPANDED aggregate (apps/api, packages/agent-runtime, packages/sdk, packages/shared-runtime, packages/model-catalog, packages/voice, packages/email, packages/agent, packages/core, packages/cli, packages/shogo-worker) and update README.md backend badge on that branch ONLY. Steps: (1) cd /tmp/shogo-ai && git checkout 'fix(coverage)/backend-unitTests' && git pull --rebase. (2) Run `bun test --coverage` across ALL 11 backend packages. For packages whose full suite hangs (agent-runtime preview-manager/warm-pool, apps/api long-running), exclude only the known-hanging files (NOT entire packages) and pin to baseline numbers for those files plus apply the precise delta from sprintCommits[] since last sync. (3) Sum linesHit / sum linesFound across all 11 packages with the comment-stripping post-processor at scripts/coverage-strip-comments.ts. Same for functions. (4) Update README.md: replace the Backend coverage badge URL with the new percentage (preserve badge style/color thresholds: ≥90 brightgreen, ≥75 yellow, ≥60 orange, else red). DO NOT TOUCH the Frontend badge — frontend is out of scope on this branch. (5) Refresh coverage/baselines/*.gaps.json and coverage/coverage-tasks.json metadata (lastUpdated, aggregate {lines,functions}, perPackage[] for all 11 packages, and the sprintCommits[] log). (6) git add README.md coverage/ && git commit -m 'docs(coverage): bump README backend → <new>% (expanded v2 aggregate)' && git push origin 'fix(coverage)/backend-unitTests'. (7) Report the before → after delta, the per-package breakdown, and how far the headline is from 100%. Do NOT push to main or any other branch. Do NOT open PRs. NEVER modify the Frontend badge.

label: Stabilize
prompt: Execute Wave A Day 1 of .shogo/plans/backend-100-coverage-finale_v2.plan.md on branch fix(coverage)/backend-unitTests ONLY: fix every pre-existing test failure across all backend packages until `bun test` returns 0 failures, 0 errors, 0 hangs. Specifically: (1) cd /tmp/shogo-ai && git checkout 'fix(coverage)/backend-unitTests' && git pull --rebase. (2) Run `bun test` in each backend package and inventory ALL failures into a short list. (3) Fix them in this order, one commit per group: (a) shared-runtime env leaks — set process.env.S3_* and KUBERNETES_* to known values in beforeEach across s3-sync.test.ts, extract-tar.test.ts, platform-pkg.test.ts; (b) shared-runtime lsp-bin-resolution.test.ts — mock node:child_process so tsc/eslint resolve deterministically; (c) sdk Playwright stray files — exclude *.spec.ts from `bun test` glob, add a separate Playwright project config; (d) sdk missing @shogo-ai/voice — extend preload-sdk-mocks.ts with a voice subpath stub; (e) agent-runtime hangs — move preview-manager and warm-pool integration tests to src/__tests__/integration/ and exclude from default `bun test`; (f) apps/api knative-project-manager core dump — diagnose with --bail and inspect crash. (4) After each group, run `bun test` in the affected package and verify zero failures. (5) git add -A && git commit -m 'test: fix <group> failures (Wave A Day 1)' && git push origin 'fix(coverage)/backend-unitTests' for each group. (6) Final report: which failures were real bugs, which were test-infra issues, and the clean `bun test` output across all 11 backend packages. Do NOT push to main or any other branch. Do NOT open PRs. Do NOT touch main.
